Since the lake starts with 100 tons of fish and the maximum population is 100 tons, if all fishermen catch too many fish this month, the fish population will decrease significantly, reducing future income. To maintain a sustainable fish population and maximize long-term income, it is best to catch a moderate amount.

If each fisherman catches 10 tons, total catch is 50 tons, leaving 50 tons in the lake. After reproduction, the fish population doubles to 100 tons, maintaining the maximum population.

Catching 10 tons this month yields $10,000 and keeps the fish population sustainable for future months.

Answer: 10

Since the lake starts with the maximum population of 100 tons, if everyone fishes too aggressively this month, the population will drop significantly, reducing future income. To maximize long-term income, I should fish a moderate amount to allow the fish population to replenish.

If I fish too little, I might miss out on immediate income. If I fish too much, the population will collapse. A balanced approach is to fish around 10-20 tons, assuming others do the same, so the total catch is sustainable.

I will choose to fish 15 tons this month to balance immediate income and sustainability.
